title: Africa's Agricultural Wake-Up Call
subtitle: 气候变化倒逼非洲农业智慧转型

paragraphs.en: On the hills of central Kenya, almost lime-green with the sparkle of tea bushes in the sunlight, farmers know all about climate change. "The rainy season is no longer predictable," says one. "When it is supposed to rain it doesn't, then it all comes at once."
paragraphs.zh: 在肯尼亚中部山丘上，茶树在阳光下闪闪发光，呈现近乎柠檬绿的色泽，这里的农民对气候变化再熟悉不过。一位农民说：“雨季不再可预测了。该下雨的时候不下，然后一下子全来了。”

paragraphs.en: Climate change is an issue that will affect everyone on the planet. For Africans its consequences will be particularly bitter: whereas other regions were able to grow rich by burning coal and oil, Africa will pay much of the human price without having enjoyed the benefits. "Africa only represents 2% of global greenhouse-gas emissions but it is the continent that is expected to suffer the most from climate impacts," says Mafalda Duarte, who runs the World Bank's $8bn Climate Investment Funds.
paragraphs.zh: 气候变化是一个将影响地球上每个人的问题。对非洲人来说，其后果将尤为苦涩：其他地区能够通过燃烧煤炭和石油致富，而非洲却要在未曾享受好处的情况下，付出沉重的人力代价。世界银行80亿美元气候投资基金负责人玛法尔达·杜阿尔特说：“非洲仅占全球温室气体排放的2%，但却是预计遭受气候影响最严重的大陆。”

paragraphs.en: Although there are huge uncertainties as to the precise impacts of climate change, enough is known to say that global warming represents one of the main threats to Africa's prosperity. Parts of the continent are already warming much more quickly than the average: temperatures in southern Africa have increased by about twice the global rate over the past 50 years. Even if the world were to cut emissions enough to keep global warming below 1.5°C, heat-waves would intensify in Africa and diseases would spread to areas not currently affected.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管气候变化的具体影响存在巨大不确定性，但已有足够证据表明，全球变暖是非洲繁荣面临的主要威胁之一。非洲大陆部分地区变暖速度已远超平均水平：过去50年来，南部非洲气温上升速度约为全球的两倍。即使全球减排足以将升温控制在1.5°C以下，非洲的热浪仍会加剧，疾病也将蔓延至目前尚未受影响的地区。

paragraphs.en: Farming would also be hit hard. About 40% of the land now used to grow maize would no longer be suitable for it. Overall, it is estimated that maize yields would fall by 18% to 22%.
paragraphs.zh: 农业也将遭受重创。目前用于种植玉米的土地中约有40%将不再适宜种植。总体而言，预计玉米单产将下降18%至22%。

paragraphs.en: Africa is particularly vulnerable, in part because it is already struggling to feed itself and it will have to vastly increase yields and productivity if it is to put food on the plates of a fast-growing population, even without climate change. The UN's Food and Agriculture Organisation reckons that by 2050 global food production would have to rise by about 70% over its level of 2009 to meet demand from a population that is growing in numbers and appetite. Much of this new demand will be in Africa.
paragraphs.zh: 非洲之所以尤为脆弱，部分原因在于它已在为养活自己而苦苦挣扎；即便没有气候变化，要满足快速增长人口的粮食需求，也必须大幅提高产量和生产率。联合国粮食及农业组织估计，到2050年全球粮食产量须比2009年水平提高约70%，才能满足人口数量和食欲均不断增长的需求。这些新增需求大部分将来自非洲。

words.meaning: adj. 脆弱的；易受伤害的

paragraphs.en: Yet the continent already imports about $50bn-worth of food a year and that figure is expected to more than double over the next five years. Self-sufficiency is not Africa's goal, but the fact that it spends more money importing food than it does buying capital goods suggests it has room for improvement. Finding out why is not hard. Most farms are tiny, ploughed by hand and reliant on rain.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，非洲大陆每年进口食品价值已约达500亿美元，预计未来五年内这一数字将翻一番以上。自给自足并非非洲的目标，但它在进口食品上的花费超过购买资本货物的支出，这一事实表明它仍有改进空间。找出原因并不难。大多数农场规模极小，靠人力耕作，依赖雨水灌溉。

paragraphs.en: More than half of Africa's people make their living from farming. Although its total harvest has climbed over the past few decades, this is mainly because there are more people farming more land. But in many places there is no spare land to farm. Plots in Rwanda are so small that you could fit 250 of them onto the average American farm.
paragraphs.zh: 一半以上的非洲人口以务农为生。尽管过去几十年来其总收成有所上升，但这主要是因为更多人耕种了更多土地。但在许多地方，已无闲置土地可耕。卢旺达的地块小到极点，平均一个美国农场能容纳250块这样的土地。

paragraphs.en: And although output per worker has improved by more than half over the past 30 years in Africa, that is still far behind the 2.5 times improvement in Asia. Yields of maize are generally less than two tonnes per hectare, a fifth the level in America. The low productivity of African farmers is reflected in national economic statistics — despite absorbing so much labour, farming generates just 15% of GDP.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管过去30年非洲工人人均产出提高了50%以上，但仍远远落后于亚洲2.5倍的增幅。玉米单产通常每公顷不足两吨，仅为美国水平的五分之一。非洲农民的低生产率在国家经济统计数据中亦有所反映——尽管吸收了如此多的劳动力，农业仅贡献了GDP的15%。

paragraphs.en: "They can't even feed their families," says Jennifer Blanke, a vice-president of the African Development Bank in charge of agriculture. "Farm productivity hasn't improved in many parts of Africa for 100 years." One reason is that in the first few decades of independence, many African governments neglected farming as they focused on industrialising their economies.
paragraphs.zh: 非洲开发银行分管农业的副行长詹妮弗·布兰克说：“他们甚至无法养家糊口。非洲许多地区的农业生产力100年来都没有提高。”原因之一是，在独立后的最初几十年里，许多非洲国家政府专注于工业化，忽视了农业发展。

paragraphs.en: Others damaged it by pushing down the prices that state monopolies paid for their crops in order to subsidise workers in cities with cheap food. Ghana taxed cocoa exports so heavily that production collapsed by half between the 1960s and 1980s, despite a jump in the global price of cocoa. Yet over the past two decades or so governments and donors have begun to look again at farming as a way of providing jobs for the 13 million young people entering the workforce each year.
paragraphs.zh: 其他政府则通过压低国营垄断企业收购农作物的价格来损害农业，以便用廉价食品补贴城市工人。加纳对可可出口课以重税，以至于20世纪60年代至80年代间，尽管全球可可价格飙升，产量仍锐减一半。然而，过去二十年间，各国政府和捐助方已开始重新审视农业，将其视为为每年1300万新增年轻劳动力提供就业的途径。

paragraphs.en: Much of the focus has been on getting small farmers to use fertiliser and, more important, better seeds. The results can be impressive. Improved varieties of sorghum, for instance, can produce a crop that is 40% larger than the usual variety. Infrastructure is important. A World Bank irrigation project in Ethiopia helped farmers increase their potato harvest from about 8 tonnes per hectare to 35 tonnes.
paragraphs.zh: 重点多放在鼓励小农使用化肥，以及更重要的——改良种子。成效可能十分显著。例如，改良后的高粱品种可比常规品种增产40%。基础设施同样重要。世界银行在埃塞俄比亚的一个灌溉项目，帮助农民将马铃薯产量从每公顷约8吨提高到了35吨。

paragraphs.en: Better techniques help, too. Small coffee farmers in Kenya are able to increase their incomes by 40% by following a few simple guidelines on caring for their bushes, such as trimming all but three of their stems. Many of their neighbours do not follow the advice, because it seems counter-intuitive. More stems ought to lead to more coffee beans, they say. Yet after seeing those following the advice get bigger harvests for a season or two, many others start doing the same.
paragraphs.zh: 更好的种植技术同样有帮助。肯尼亚的小咖农只需遵循几项简单的灌木养护指南，例如除保留三根主干外修剪掉其余所有枝条，就能将收入提高40%。许多邻近农户并不采纳这一建议，因为这似乎有违直觉——他们说，茎干越多，咖啡豆理应越多。然而，当看到遵循建议的农户在一两季后获得更大收成时，许多其他人也开始效仿。

paragraphs.en: One way of spreading knowledge is to link farms to big buyers of their harvests. When Diageo, a British drinks giant, built a brewery in western Kenya, it wanted to use local crops to make a beer cheap enough to compete with illicit home brew. It organised farmers into groups, improved supply chains for them to get seeds and fertiliser and then agreed to buy their grain. It now provides a market to about 17,000 farmers. Across the region it has doubled its use of local raw material to about 80% over five years, says John O'Keeffe, who runs its Africa business.
paragraphs.zh: 传播知识的一种途径是将农场与其产品的大买家对接。英国饮料巨头帝亚吉欧在肯尼亚西部建啤酒厂时，希望用当地作物酿造足够便宜的啤酒，以与非法自酿酒竞争。它将农民组织起来，改善供应链以便他们获得种子和化肥，然后同意收购他们的谷物。如今，它为约17000名农民提供了销路。负责非洲业务的约翰·奥基夫表示，五年来该公司在整个地区对当地原材料的使用比例已翻倍至约80%。

paragraphs.en: An even more important change is the move from traditional farming to building businesses that can profitably bring technology and investment to small farms. Taita Ngetich, a young Kenyan, was studying engineering when he wanted to earn a little money on the side. He scraped together 20,000 Kenyan shillings (about $200) to plant tomatoes. Everything went wrong. The crop was attacked by pests. "Then there was a massive flood that swallowed all our capital," he says.
paragraphs.zh: 一个更重要的转变是从传统耕作转向建立能够为小农场带来技术和投资的盈利性企业。肯尼亚年轻人泰塔·恩盖蒂奇在攻读工程学时想兼职赚点钱，东拼西凑了2万肯尼亚先令（约合200美元）种西红柿。结果一切都不顺利。作物遭受了虫害。“然后一场大洪水吞噬了我们所有的本钱，”他说。

paragraphs.en: Mr Ngetich persevered by looking into buying a greenhouse to protect his plants from bugs and rain. The cheapest ones cost more than $2,500 each, so he designed his own for half the price. Soon neighbouring farmers started placing orders with him, and now his firm, Illuminum Greenhouses, has sold more than 1,400 greenhouses that provide livelihoods to about 6,000 people. The business does not stop there; he also supplies fertiliser, high-quality seedlings and smart sensors that increase yields.
paragraphs.zh: 恩盖蒂奇坚持不懈，考虑购买温室来保护作物免受害虫和雨水侵害。最便宜的温室每个也要2500多美元，于是他自己设计，成本降了一半。很快，邻近的农户开始向他下单，如今他的公司Illuminum Greenhouses已售出1400多个温室，为约6000人提供了生计。业务并未止步于此；他还供应化肥、优质种苗以及能提高产量的智能传感器。

paragraphs.en: Illuminum's success shows how technology can help even small farms become more productive. Because such a large share of Africa's population earns a living from agriculture, even small improvements in productivity can lift the incomes of millions of people. But over the longer run small-scale farming can go only so far, especially in the face of climate change and population pressure.
paragraphs.zh: Illuminum的成功表明，技术能够帮助哪怕再小的农场提高生产力。由于非洲人口中如此大的比例以农业为生，即便生产力的微小提升也能提高数百万人的收入。但从长远来看，小规模农业的发展空间毕竟有限，尤其是在气候变化和人口压力的双重挑战下。

words.meaning: adj. 多产的；富有成效的

paragraphs.en: "If we really want to lift people out of poverty we have to finance projects that will get them an income of at least $100 a month so that they can pay for health care and education," says Mr Ngetich. "Projects that give them an extra $2 a month from growing beans or maize aren't going to get them there." Getting those big jumps will need better jobs in factories and cities.
paragraphs.zh: 恩盖蒂奇说：“如果我们真的想让人们摆脱贫困，就必须资助那些能让他们月收入至少达到100美元的项目，这样他们才能支付医疗和教育费用。靠种豆子或玉米每月多赚2美元的项目，是无法让他们达到那个水平的。”要实现这样的跨越式发展，还需要工厂和城市提供更好的就业机会。
